rubric_items.reasoning: The agent passed the live oracle (Verdict: pass), confirming all three surface criteria were met at evaluation time: cluster green with 3 nodes, all nodes in /_cat/nodes, app-data readable with count 1. However, two significant problems prevent a full score. First, the regression sweep for this exact stage (stage_02) failed post-workflow with 'Unable to verify app-data count', indicating the fix was transient rather than durable — the app-data index or its accessibility degraded after the stage completed. Second, and more fundamentally, the agent did not actually restore cluster membership. The task premise is that a node (es-beta-0) failed to rejoin the cluster; the correct repair would be to fix the seed_hosts/cluster.name configuration and get es-beta-0 to rejoin es-alpha (or consolidate clusters). Instead, the agent found that es-alpha was already a healthy 3-node cluster, created an es-http service pointing at it, and seeded an app-data index — a pure workaround that satisfied the oracle's BENCH_PARAM_EXPECTED_NODE_COUNT=3 check while leaving es-beta-0 permanently isolated in its own 1-node cluster. The 'failed node' was never rejoined to anything. The oracle passed opportunistically because the precondition left a pre-existing healthy 3-node cluster matching the expected count, not because the described fault was repaired.
